title: Arianespace to launch Katalyst servicing spacecraft
summary: Arianespace will launch a geosynchronous orbit servicing spacecraft called Katalyst for Katalyst Space Technologies aboard an Ariane 6 rocket in 2027. This marks a significant step in in-space servicing and assembly missions.

title: York Space rides defense demand as procurement changes cloud outlook
summary: York Space Systems is leveraging increased defense demand and commercial contracts to navigate procurement changes in Space Development Agency-led programs. However, shifts in procurement policies may pose challenges to its outlook.

title: The software behind Artemis, Gateway and America’s defense in space deserves its own strategy
summary: The article highlights the overlooked importance of software in national space infrastructure, including missions like Artemis and Gateway, and argues for a dedicated strategy to address it.

title: Lunar lander developers say they are ready to meet anticipated increased NASA demand
summary: Two lunar lander companies claim they are prepared to meet NASA's expected rise in lunar landing missions, though specifics on execution remain unclear.

title: Swissto12 to build small optical relay GEO satellite for Space Compass
summary: Swissto12 will construct a small optical relay geostationary satellite for Space Compass using its HummingSat platform. This marks a significant step in optical communication technology for space applications.

title: SpaceX offers details on orbital data center satellites
summary: SpaceX CEO Elon Musk shared technical details about the company's plans to deploy a constellation of orbital data center satellites. Financial specifics were not disclosed.

title: Some European Launcher Challenge funding remains in limbo
summary: Nearly 140 million euros allocated by ESA member states for a launch vehicle development program remains unallocated and risks being lost. The delay threatens progress in European space launch capabilities.

title: Artemis 2 returns to the pad for April launch attempt
summary: The Artemis 2 launch vehicle and spacecraft have been moved back to the launch pad for a potential launch attempt in April. This marks a key milestone in NASA's preparations for the mission.

title: Officina Stellare wins $2 million contract for lasercom ground station in Spain
summary: Officina Stellare secured a $2 million contract to design and build an optical ground station in Spain for laser and quantum-encrypted space communications. The project is in collaboration with the Institute of Photonic Sciences (ICFO) in Barcelona.

title: Rocket Lab launches eighth Synspective radar imaging satellite
summary: Rocket Lab launched the eighth radar imaging satellite for Synspective on March 20. The mission marks another step in expanding Synspective's Earth observation capabilities.

title: Triangulum Galaxy dazzles in psychedelic color | Space photo of the day for March 23, 2026
summary: Astronomers captured a vibrant new image of the Triangulum Galaxy, showcasing intricate gas clouds among its 40 billion stars. This psychedelic space photo was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for March 23, 2026.

title: Northern lights dazzle worldwide as strong G3 geomagnetic storm sparks stunning show (photos)
summary: A powerful G3 geomagnetic storm caused the northern lights to be visible far beyond the poles, creating a stunning display worldwide. The event was captured in numerous photos shared globally.

title: Are mysterious 'Little Red Dots' discovered by the James Webb Space Telescope actually baby galaxies under construction?
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has discovered mysterious 'Little Red Dots' that may be either baby galaxies or globular clusters in formation. The findings challenge previous assumptions about these objects being baby black holes.

title: Could our universe exist because black holes ate up all the antimatter?
summary: A new theory suggests primordial black holes formed during the Big Bang may have consumed antimatter, explaining why matter dominates the universe. This hypothesis challenges traditional explanations for the matter-antimatter asymmetry.

title: 10 iconic William Shatner 'Star Trek' moments to celebrate his 95th birthday
summary: The article celebrates William Shatner's 95th birthday by highlighting ten iconic moments from his 'Star Trek' career. It focuses on his memorable scenes rather than space industry news.

title: What if the next great astronomer isn't human? How AI is revolutionizing our study of the cosmos
summary: AI is transforming astronomy by enhancing human researchers' capabilities, potentially uncovering new cosmic discoveries. The collaboration between human astronomers and AI is still in its early stages but shows immense promise.

title: 'This is really intolerable': Astronomers protest giant orbiting mirror project and SpaceX's million AI satellites
summary: Astronomers are protesting a proposed project involving giant orbiting mirrors and SpaceX's million AI satellites, warning that it would severely disrupt the night sky. Experts argue that these constellations would destroy humanity's view of the stars as we have known it for millennia.

title: Russian cargo spacecraft suffers glitch after launching toward International Space Station
summary: Russia's Progress 94 cargo spacecraft experienced a docking antenna deployment failure after launching toward the International Space Station on March 22, complicating its planned arrival.

title: We chat to legendary space shuttle commander Eileen Collins about her new documentary, 'Spacewoman' (interview)
summary: A new documentary titled 'Spacewoman' highlights the achievements of former space shuttle commander Eileen Collins. The interview discusses her pioneering career as an astronaut.

title: NASA to Provide Update on Implementation of National Space Policy
summary: NASA will host a public event to discuss its implementation of the National Space Policy and plans for returning to the Moon by 2028. The event is scheduled for March 24 at NASA Headquarters in Washington.

title: NASA’s Hubble Revisits Crab Nebula to Track 25 Years of Expansion
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has revisited the Crab Nebula, 25 years after its first observations, to track its expansion. The new images provide detailed insights into the supernova remnant's evolution over time.

title: Transformational Tools and Technologies Resources
summary: NASA has initiated Transformational Tools and Technologies Resources Vision Studies, Analysis of Alternatives (AoA) Studies, and White Papers to explore advanced technologies and methodologies for future space missions. These studies aim to identify innovative solutions and strategic directions for the agency's long-term goals.

title: NASA’s X-59 Experimental Supersonic Aircraft Makes Second Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 experimental supersonic aircraft completed its second flight, marking the start of numerous test flights planned for 2026. Despite an early landing due to a technical issue, the team gathered valuable data and ensured a safe landing.

title: Hangar One Restoration Project
summary: The restoration of Hangar One, a historic aviation landmark in the San Francisco Bay Area, has been completed. The project was led by Planetary Ventures.

title: NASA Selects University Finalists for Technology Concepts Competition
summary: NASA selected 14 university teams as finalists for the 2026 RASC-AL Competition, challenging students to design innovative concepts for lunar, Martian, and deep-space missions.

title: How Open NASA Data on Comet 3I/ATLAS Will Power Tomorrow’s Discoveries
summary: NASA's public data archives will preserve observations of the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS, which is leaving our solar system. Over a dozen NASA missions contributed data to study the rare comet, marking it as the third known interstellar visitor.

title: Smiles and Spacesuits
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams undergoes spacesuit fit verification inside the ISS's Quest airlock to ensure airtightness and mobility. The procedure also assesses comfort and mitigates safety risks.

title: NASA Exploration, Science Inspire “Project Hail Mary” Film
summary: NASA's upcoming Artemis II mission, the first crewed flight under the Artemis program, coincides with the premiere of the film 'Project Hail Mary,' blending real-life space exploration with science fiction.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 successfully launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from the repaired Baikonur Cosmodrome pad. The mission follows repairs made to the launch site after the previous Soyuz MS-28 launch.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center in space. This marks a significant expansion of the company's space infrastructure ambitions.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone as the agency advances toward its crewed lunar mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. The test brings the next Starship launch one step closer to reality.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of launches is scheduled with nine launches expected, including missions by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced NEO Hunter, a hybrid planetary defense concept aimed at protecting Earth from near-Earth objects. The mission concept highlights innovative approaches to planetary defense.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans to send humans to the Moon and Mars, highlighted by the transformation of its Starfactory facility. The company is repurposing the facility from steel production to support the development of its Starship spacecraft.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: New crew members arrived at the International Space Station (ISS) in February, following the early departure of Crew-11 in January due to a medical concern. The month was marked by new science experiments and crew activities aboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to dominate another busy week of missions
summary: Eight launches are scheduled worldwide this week, with SpaceX's Falcon 9 dominating the launch schedule. The post highlights the busy week ahead in space missions.

title: SpaceX’s Enters a new era with Booster 19 Testing on Pad 2
summary: SpaceX has begun testing Booster 19 on Pad 2, marking a new phase in operations since its last flight 147 days ago. This development signifies progress in SpaceX's reusable rocket technology.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry event or development, but specific details were not provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication commemorates significant milestones in space exploration and science.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team has confirmed the spacecraft is operational and has 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato exoplanet-hunting mission is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, will launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will release ECOS version 6 software on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for testing robotic and spacecraft maneuvers. This initiative aims to engage young researchers in practical space science and technology development.

title: Contrivian Launches New Product Bundling Amazon Leo & Starlink
summary: Contrivian has launched a new product that bundles satellite connectivity services from Amazon's Project Kuiper (Leo) and SpaceX's Starlink. This offering allows customers to use both networks without choosing one over the other.

title: SpaceX v. Amazon Heats Up at the FCC
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are engaged in a regulatory dispute at the FCC regarding their satellite constellations. The public dueling highlights the competitive tension between the two companies in the satellite communications sector.

title: Kayhan Space Launches Satcat Terminal
summary: Kayhan Space has launched a new Satcat Terminal business, which could benefit liberal arts majors. The initiative aims to expand access to satellite data and applications.

title: Inside Austria’s Small But Mighty Space Ecosystem
summary: Austria's space industry is experiencing growth with increased funding and startup activity. The country is emerging as a notable player in the global space ecosystem.

title: Portal, Paladin Team On Debris Removal Service
summary: Portal and Paladin are collaborating on an operational debris removal service, emphasizing the importance of active debris management for critical satellite infrastructure. The initiative aims to transition debris removal from experimental to practical applications.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its SR75 suborbital rocket, with the second flight scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first contract in Japan with NEC Corporation to purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission next year. This deal marks Apex's entry into the Japanese space market.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket is undergoing modifications to improve its nozzle and solid rocket booster, with a planned launch this summer. These changes were in development before a recent anomaly and are now being expedited.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round to reduce reliance on satellite infrastructure. The funding will support their efforts to develop alternative navigation technologies.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics secured a deal with South Korea's national security sector, marking another success in its global expansion strategy. The agreement highlights the growing collaboration between NanoAvionics and the South Korean space industry.

title: Spire Targets 50% Growth in 2026 After Adjustments in 2025
summary: Spire Global aims for a 50% revenue growth in 2026 after divesting its maritime business and reducing debt in 2025, as announced by CEO Theresa Condor.

title: Eutelsat CEO Fallacher Talks OneWeb Gen 2, Global Competition, and Sovereign Demand
summary: Eutelsat Group CEO Jean-François Fallacher discusses the company's strategic focus on OneWeb Gen 2, global competition, and sovereign demand amid its ongoing transformation.

title: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space Plan Repeatable Debris Removal Missions
summary: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space are collaborating to provide commercial debris removal missions through a recurring service model called Debris Removal as a Service (DRAAS). This partnership aims to address the growing issue of space debris with regular, commercialized cleanup operations.
company: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space

title: SLI Purchases Two Small GEO Satellites From ReOrbit
summary: SLI has acquired two small Geostationary Orbit (GEO) communication satellites from ReOrbit for 150 million euros ($172.8 million). The deal was announced in a joint statement by both companies.

title: Comtech Delivers First Set of Digital Modems to US Army
summary: Comtech delivered the first set of Enterprise Digital Intermediate Frequency Multi-Carrier (EDIM) modems to the U.S. Army under a $48.6 million contract signed in 2023. The company announced the delivery on Thursday.

title: Latest White House Cybersecurity Strategy Talks Crypto, AI, Quantum
summary: The White House released President Trump’s Cyber Strategy for America in mid-March, focusing on securing critical infrastructure and maintaining superiority in emerging technologies like crypto, AI, and quantum.

title: Getting Better Outcomes In Space: How Will the UK Embrace the Challenge?
summary: A panel at Space-Comm in London discussed the security of UK space assets and strategic stability from government and industry perspectives. The event explored how the UK can improve outcomes in space amid growing challenges.

title: SpiderOak Wins New UAS Systems Contract
summary: SpiderOak has secured a new contract with the U.S. Army Unmanned Aircraft Systems (PMO UAS) to conduct supply chain analysis and cybersecurity assessments for multiple vendors. The contract focuses on enhancing security and reliability in UAS systems.

title: Ukraine Ambassador to UK Talks Future Warfare
summary: Ukrainian Ambassador to the UK discussed the future of warfare at an event in London, marking four years since Russia's invasion. The speech was organized by Chatham House.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has filed a proposal with the FCC to host an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites, asserting that it won't cause interference. The company claims the plan aligns with existing L-band coordination.
